Comments: Constructed with the Reporting Organisation unique identifier and the organisation’s chosen activity number.
Unique identifier and name of organisation reporting the activity.
Meaningful description of the activity (minimum short paragraph).
Planned start and end dates of the activity.

Funding - the organisation(s) funding the project.

Implementing - the organisation carrying out the actual work of the project.

Accountable - the organisation responsible for managing the delivery of the project.

The country or countries that benefit from the activity – i.e. where impact is felt. These should be eligible for Official Development Assistance (ODA) and may differ from the location of the activity.

For example, activities undertaken in the territory of the donor country (e.g. research scholarships, training courses) should be assigned to individual recipient countries where the services are performed for the benefit of those countries.

Regions can be specified instead of, or as well as, individual countries if needed. When using percentages, all percentages across countries and regions must add up to 100%.

Incoming Funds and Outgoing Disbursements are mandatory. 

Expenditure, Incoming and Outgoing Commitments to include if applicable.
Contact address and email for the reporting organisation. This can be a generic enquiry email address.
Data on results and indicators of the project. This can include links to published documents.
Location of the activity or its beneficiaries on a sub-national level.
Documents relating to the activity including project proposal, Theory of Change, reports, pictures, project updates etc.
The geographical scope of the activity: regional, national, subnational, etc.
An internal reference number for the activity (if applicable).
Another separately reported IATI activity that is related to this one.
The legacy data element allows for the reporting of values held in a field in the reporting organisation’s system which is similar, but not identical to an IATI element.
Information on the conditions for the project.
